key_points: Equality is a key feature of Indian democracy and influences all aspects of its functioning.
Universal Adult Franchise means that all adult citizens (18 and above) have the right to vote regardless of their wealth or community.
The Indian Constitution recognizes every person as equal, meaning every individual in the country is equal before the law.
Despite being a democracy, inequality still exists in India in the form of the caste system, religion, and economic status.
One of the main ways the government tries to implement equality is through laws and government programs/schemes.
examples: Kanta's Story: A domestic worker who stands in the same line to vote as her employer, showing political equality.
Midday Meal Scheme: A program started in Tamil Nadu to provide cooked lunch to children in government schools.
Omprakash Valmiki: A famous Dalit writer who wrote about the discrimination he faced in school in his book 'Joothan'.
Ansari Family: A couple who faced difficulty in renting an apartment because of their religion.
formulas: Universal Adult Franchise = 1 Person : 1 Vote : 1 Value
exam_tips: Remember that Tamil Nadu was the first state in India to start the Midday Meal scheme in 2001.
Understand that 'Dignity' refers to the thinking of oneself and other persons as worthy of respect.
Note that the Civil Rights Act of 1964 in the USA prohibited discrimination on the basis of race, religion, or national origin.
Identify that Article 15 of the Indian Constitution prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth.
